topic.what: Certified malware detection framework using randomized smoothing with feature ablation and targeted noise injection on EMBER features
topic.how: Trains LightGBM base classifier; generates augmented malicious variants via 80% feature group retention and 10% Gaussian noise injection; applies majority voting over 50 perturbations with Wilson score interval for robustness certification
topic.why_matters: Provides formal L2 robustness guarantees against metamorphic evasion attacks rather than empirical-only defenses

characteristics.what_it_measures: Classification accuracy on clean test data
characteristics.context: Smoothed classifier maintains high accuracy on clean data
Smoothed classifier significantly more robust to noise
SC maintains near-clean accuracy even at high noise levels
Robustness gap increases with noise intensity

characteristics.what_it_measures: Certified L2 perturbation radius for provable stability
characteristics.value: R = σ · Φ^-1(plower) where plower computed via Wilson score interval at 99.9% confidence
characteristics.context: Formal certification mechanism based on majority voting distribution

good_for: PE malware detection on Windows executables
Formal robustness guarantees against metamorphic evasion
not_for: Real-time detection systems requiring low latency
Systems without computational budget for multiple perturbations
requires: Sufficient computational resources for 50 perturbation evaluations
Sufficient training data for augmented training

novelty: Adaptation of randomized smoothing to discrete PE feature space via feature grouping
Combination of group-wise ablation (80% retention) with targeted Gaussian noise injection (ρ=0.3)
Formal robustness certification using Wilson score interval at 99.9% confidence
Empirical evaluation against PyMetaEngine-generated metamorphic variants
open_problems: Extension to other file formats (ELF, PDF, APK)
Integration with deep learning architectures (MalConv) beyond LightGBM
